text.title: rag Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for production RAG: chunking + metadata, hybrid default, rerank top-20→5, context order, answer-only-from-context with citations, MRR/faithfulness gate.

rule.statement: Pick chunk_size by document type (512 Q&A / 1024 technical / 1500 papers) with 20% overlap; attach metadata (filename, page, section, ingestion-date) at index time. Tune chunk size empirically on a representative query sample.
rule.rationale: Chunk size balances precision (smaller) vs context completeness (larger). Metadata cannot be added retroactively without full re-embed.

rule.statement: Use hybrid search (BM25 + vector) by default; fall back to pure vector only after benchmarking shows no improvement on the labeled eval set.
rule.rationale: Pure semantic search misses exact technical terms, codes, and rare names; hybrid adds BM25 coverage at minimal latency on modern stores.

rule.statement: Use a cross-encoder reranker for all production retrieval. Retrieve top-20, rerank to top-5 (or top-K determined by token budget). Token budget = top_K × avg_chunk_tokens must stay well below context limit minus system prompt.
rule.rationale: Vector similarity is calibrated for recall, not precision. Cross-encoder rerank pays 100-300ms for a meaningful precision lift. Context overflow drops middle chunks.

rule.statement: Place highest-scored chunks first in the prompt context. Never randomise order.
rule.rationale: LLMs exhibit "lost in the middle" — earlier and last positions get more attention. Highest relevance must lead.

rule.statement: Instruct the model to answer ONLY from the provided context, with an explicit fallback "If the context does not contain the answer, say so." Require citations in [Source: filename, page N] format and validate them against retrieved chunk metadata post-generation.
rule.rationale: Without this instruction the model leaks training knowledge confidently. Unvalidated citations are unverified assertions; validation catches hallucinated citations.

rule.statement: Before launching, require MRR@10 > 0.7 on the test set AND faithfulness > 0.9 (RAGAS or LLM judge). Re-evaluate after any chunk, embedding, or reranker change.
rule.rationale: Below these thresholds, the system ships hallucinations or wrong answers at unacceptable rates. Re-eval after changes catches regressions before users do.

rule.statement: Skip RAG when the corpus fits a single context window AND token cost is acceptable; or when questions are general-knowledge with no domain corpus dependency.
rule.rationale: Context-stuffing beats RAG at small scale; general-knowledge questions don't need retrieval.
